exercises.title: Measure collisions
exercises.description: Hash a few thousand strings into a small bucket array and count how many buckets collide.
exercises.expected_outcome: You should see collisions rise as the table gets smaller or the key set gets more biased.

exercises.title: Compare lookup paths
exercises.description: Time a linear search over a list versus a dictionary lookup for the same key set.
exercises.expected_outcome: Dictionary lookup should stay near constant time while linear search scales with n.

exercises.title: Implement chaining
exercises.description: Build a small hash table with separate chaining and support insert, get, and delete.
exercises.expected_outcome: Lookups should stay simple even when several keys land in the same bucket.

exercises.title: Implement linear probing
exercises.description: Write an open-addressed table with linear probing and tombstones for deletes.
exercises.expected_outcome: Probes should advance until an empty or matching slot is found.

exercises.title: Build a consistent hash ring
exercises.description: Model node placement with virtual replicas and show how keys move when a node is added or removed.
exercises.expected_outcome: Only a small subset of keys should remap after each membership change.

exercises.title: Benchmark load factor tradeoffs
exercises.description: Measure lookup and insert latency as the table approaches different load factors.
exercises.expected_outcome: Performance should worsen as the table fills and collision cost grows.
